```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  ell0 <- -sample(80:250, 1)
  delta <- sample(seq(0.8, 4.0, 0.2), 1)
  aitken <- sample(seq(0.20, 0.80, 0.05), 1)
  ell1 <- ell0 + delta
  ell2 <- ell1 + aitken * delta
  ell_inf <- ell1 + (ell2 - ell1)/(1 - aitken)
  vals <- round(c(ell_inf,
                  ell2,
                  ell2 + (ell2 - ell1)/(1 - aitken),
                  ell1 + (ell1 - ell0)/(1 - aitken),
                  ell2 + aitken * (ell2 - ell1)), 3)
  if (length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.3f$", vals)
```

Question
========
Três log-verossimilhanças observadas consecutivas de um EM são
$$
\ell^{(t-2)}=`r sprintf("%.3f", ell0)`,\qquad
\ell^{(t-1)}=`r sprintf("%.3f", ell1)`,\qquad
\ell^{(t)}=`r sprintf("%.3f", ell2)`.
$$
Usando a aceleração de Aitken
$$
a^{(t)}=\frac{\ell^{(t)}-\ell^{(t-1)}}
{\ell^{(t-1)}-\ell^{(t-2)}},\qquad
\ell_\infty^{(t)}=\ell^{(t-1)}+
\frac{\ell^{(t)}-\ell^{(t-1)}}{1-a^{(t)}},
$$
qual é a estimativa de $\ell_\infty^{(t)}$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
Neste caso,
$$
a^{(t)}=\frac{`r sprintf("%.3f", ell2 - ell1)`}
{`r sprintf("%.3f", ell1 - ell0)`}=`r sprintf("%.3f", aitken)`.
$$
Logo,
$$
\ell_\infty^{(t)}=`r sprintf("%.3f", ell1)`+
\frac{`r sprintf("%.3f", ell2 - ell1)`}
{1-`r sprintf("%.3f", aitken)`}=`r sprintf("%.3f", ell_inf)`.
$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap8S3Q4_aceleracao_Aitken
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
